title: "How to read it in a paper" checklist deck (journal-club/tumor-board ready)
context: Each individual explainer includes a howToReadInAPaper checklist section, but clinicians attending a journal club or tumor board need a compact, standalone checklist deck they can print or reference without reading the full explainer for each concept. This M2 task synthesizes the per-explainer checklists from at minimum hr-004, km-006, and ci-pvalue-012 into a short, usable reference deck organized by statistical concept — suitable as a journal-club facilitation tool or tumor-board reading aid. This does not introduce new statistical content; it is a synthesis and reformat of approved, biostatistician-signed-off checklist content.
objective: Compile and publish a 'how to read it in a paper' checklist deck, synthesizing the per-explainer howToReadInAPaper checklists into a compact, standalone reference document organized by statistical concept, suitable for journal-club and tumor-board use.
acceptanceCriteria: The checklist deck covers at minimum the howToReadInAPaper checklists from hr-004 (hazard ratio), km-006 (KM curves and censoring), and ci-pvalue-012 (CI vs p-values); additional checklists from other signed-off explainers may be included.
Each checklist card in the deck is derived entirely from biostatistician-signed-off explainer content; no new statistical claims are introduced in the deck itself.
The deck is formatted as a compact, practical reference (e.g. one concept per page/card) — printable, accessible, and usable without specialized tools.
Every item in the deck has a sourceRef traceable to its originating explainer; the deck references the explainers it synthesizes.
The structural 'educational, not clinical advice' framing is present in the deck header/footer; no treatment-advice content.
The deck is CC-BY-4.0 with attribution to sources; figures (if any) are alt-texted and colorblind-safe; readability target met.
Biostatistician statsSignoff and clinical-framing review are recorded for the deck (reviewing for any unintended new claims or framings introduced during synthesis).
DCO signed-off; accessible formats (e.g. Markdown and PDF) committed.
output: A published CC-BY-4.0 'how to read it in a paper' checklist deck covering at minimum HR, KM/censoring, and CI/p-value topics, formatted for journal-club and tumor-board use, with biostatistician sign-off confirming no new statistical claims are introduced.
